Microwave the pre-cooked quinoa.
Serve the curry on top of the rice and enjoy!

name: Honey Mustard Salmon
ingredients: salt and ground black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Grease a baking sheet with olive oil.

Place salmon fillets on the baking sheet. Whisk mustard and honey together in a small bowl. Brush some of the mustard mixture all over the fillets, reserving some of the mixture in the bowl. Sprinkle marjoram, salt, and pepper over fillets.
Bake in the preheated oven until fish flakes easily with a fork, 10 to 15 minutes.

Serve on warmed plates and top with the remaining mustard mixture. Pair your salmon with a salad, cooked veggies, reheated leftover veggies. Add in extra grains like rice or quinoa, or toast some gluten free bread if you’d like. Enjoy!

name: Quinoa Salad with Fresh Mint and Parsley
ingredients: 2 cups quinoa, precooked (cook by scratch in advance or buy it precooked)
3 medium scallions, or one bunch green onions, chopped
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
steps: In a large bowl, combine the quinoa, nuts, parsley, and mint.

In a separate bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, and garlic salt, then pour over the precooked quinoa. Gently toss this dressing together with the quinoa to disperse evenly and combine well.
Season with a generous amount of salt and pepper.
Gently toss, and taste to see if more salt and pepper is needed. Enjoy immediately or store in the refrigerator for 15 minutes to chill and let the flavors mix before eating.

name: Crab, Chilli, and Herb Omelette
ingredients: 1 tbsp crème fraîche, or substitute with a full-fat yogurt or dairy-free yogurt
2 eggs Click here for egg replacements
herb and leaf salad, to serve
optional: gluten free chilli sauce, to serve
steps: Mix the crab, chilli, herbs and crème fraîche and season well.

Beat the eggs with some seasoning until pale. Heat 1 tsp oil in a small frying pan, and pour in the eggs, making a thin omelette. When the omelette is just set, tip the crab mixture in, fold in half and warm through for a minute. Serve with an herby salad and chilli sauce if you’d like.

name: Balsamic Roasted Broccoli and Red Pepper Grilled Cheese
ingredients: ½ pound broccoli, or 8 ounces broccoli florets
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 425 degrees F. Remove the florets from the broccoli stems and slice into bite-sized pieces. Slice the bell pepper and onion into strips, all roughly the same size.

Transfer the prepared veggies to a large, rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle on the olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Add the salt and toss until all of the ingredients are evenly combined. Arrange the veggies in a single layer on the baking sheet. Sprinkle lightly with red pepper flakes and black pepper. Bake until the veggies are tender and deeply caramelized, about 20 minutes, tossing halfway.

To prepare your sandwiches: Spread a very thin layer of Dijon mustard on one slice of bread. Top the mustard with a heavy sprinkling of shredded cheese, then as much roasted vegetables as you can reasonably fit on top. Top with more cheese and place another piece of bread on top. Repeat for the second sandwich.

Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Melt a generous pat of butter on the skillet. Carefully place one sandwich on one side of the pan, then the other sandwich on the other side. Cover the pan with a lid or a baking pan to encourage the cheese to melt. Let the sandwich cook until the bottom side is golden and the cheese is mostly melted. Use a spatula to carefully flip the sandwiches over to cook until both sides are nice and golden and cheese is melted through.

name: Salmon Burgers with Slaw
ingredients: 2 eggs Click here for egg replacements
¼ cup chopped fresh herbs, like chives, parsley, or dill a squeeze of lemon juice
steps: Flake the salmon apart. Mix all burger ingredients together and form into 3 large or 4 medium patties.

Heat olive oil over medium heat, ideally in a nonstick skillet. Fry the burgers for a few minutes on each side until golden brown and crispy. Place on a paper towel lined plate and sprinkle with salt.
While the burgers are cooking, mix all slaw ingredients together. Taste and adjust.
Serve up those hot salmon burgers on a bed of creamy slaw. I top mine with a dollop of extra yogurt and a swizzle of oil and more herbs. Can’t stop me.

name: Orange and Allium Salad
steps: In a small bowl, whisk the ingredients for the dressing.
On a serving platter or individual plates, arrange greens, oranges and onion. Drizzle with dressing. Sprinkle with cheese and almonds. Enjoy!

name: Tuna Burgers with Sesame Mayo and Avocado
ingredients: 24 oz chunk light tuna in water
2 large eggs Click here for egg replacements
2 tbsp mayonnaise, or substitute vegan mayo
steps: Drain tuna. Reserve the liquid.

Place tuna in a large bowl and add the onions, eggs, and bread crumbs. Season with salt and pepper and mix well. Add reserved liquid and continue mixing until mixture has a uniform texture that holds together. Shape into patties and set aside.
In a separate bowl, add mayonnaise and sesame oil and mix well. Set aside.
Cook tuna patties in batches in an oiled skillet over medium-low heat for approximately 3 minutes per side, turning once.
Cut and pit the avocados. Scrape flesh into a bowl. Add ¼ teaspoon salt and mash with the back of a fork.
Prepare buns and stack tomatoes, tuna patties, tomato, avocado, and sesame mayonnaise. Serve hot, along with a side salad or cooked veggies. Enjoy!

name: Ratatouille For Two
ingredients: Dash of oregano (can use thyme or marjoram as well)
steps: Co-Chef 1: Preheat oven to 350° F. In a medium cast-iron skillet, spread tomato sauce evenly across the bottom of the skillet. Co-chef 2: Split up the veggies so each of you have about half of the veggies to slice. Coin flip to see who has to slice the onion!

Both co-chefs: Using a sharp knife, slice your veggies into thin rounds. Who can slice the thinnest veggie round? Winner gets a kiss :) When all the veggies have been sliced, arrange the veggie rounds into small stacks.
Co-Chef 1: Lay all the veggie stacks sideways across the marinara sauce until the skillet is filled. Co-Chef 2: Drizzle olive oil over the veggies, and then sprinkle on the oregano, salt, and pepper.
Bake for 60 minutes. Serve hot on its own or with pasta or toasted bread.

name: Week 1 Meal Prep
ingredients: 2 pounds boneless skinless chicken thighs, or substitute one block of extra-firm tofu
3 tbsp sesame oil, or substitute avocado oil or coconut oil
1 tbsp soy sauce, or substitute coconut aminos
1 tbsp chili garlic sauce or red chili paste
steps: Preheat the oven to 425°F. If you’ll be using tofu instead of chicken, set the tofu to drain by placing paper towels on either side of the block, setting the block on a plate, and stacking heavy plates or weights on top of the block to press the liquid out. If you’ll be using chicken, take the chicken thighs out of their packaging and pat them dry.

While the oven is preheating, make the miso-ginger sauce: In a food processor or blender, mix the miso paste, fresh ginger, garlic, zested lemon, oil, rice vinegar, soy sauce, and chili garlic sauce until they form a saucy red paste. Combine the sauce with the chicken or tofu in a bowl and toss to coat, then set aside. Then use the rest of your time while the oven preheats to chop and mince the onion and garlic for the Moroccan Spiced Chickpea Bowls.

If you’re using tofu, slice the block to make bite-sized cubes. Arrange the chicken thighs or tofu cubes in an even layer on a baking sheet. Once the oven is preheated, set the sheet in the oven to bake for about 25-30 minutes, flipping the chicken thighs or tofu cubes over about half way through. Once cooked, store the chicken or tofu in the fridge.

While the chicken or tofu cooks, keep prepping for the Moroccan Spiced Chickpea Bowls. Heat about 2 tbsp of oil in a large frying pan over medium heat until simmering. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft and fragrant, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic, harissa, tomato paste, cumin, paprika, and cinnamon, season with salt and pepper, and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in the chickpeas and tomatoes with their juices. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes. When they’re finished cooking, store them in the fridge.

While the chickpeas simmer, start cooking the brown rice for the Moroccan Spiced Chickpea Bowls and the Brown Rice and Boiled Egg Salad. Start by rinsing 1½ cups of brown rice (¾ for the chickpea bowls, and ¾ for the boiled egg salad). Then add it to a medium saucepan with 3¾ cups water and a pinch of salt. Stir to break up any clumps. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover with a lid to let simmer for about 45 minutes. Then turn the heat all the way off and let it rest in the pot for another 10 minutes. Transfer the rice to a container and store in the fridge.

Boil the eggs for the Moroccan Spiced Chickpea Bowls and Brown Rice and Boiled Egg Salad. If you don’t eat eggs, you may skip this step or use this time to make an egg replacement recipe Place 8 eggs in a big pot and fill with cold water until the eggs are covered by about an inch of water. Bring the water to a rolling boil. As soon as the water starts to boil, turn the heat off, cover the pot with a lid, and remove the pot from the burner. Let the eggs stay in the covered pot for about 10 minutes. Then remove them and place them in a bowl of ice water for about 1 minute. Remove the eggs and store them in the fridge. You can always put them back into the egg carton- just make sure the carton is labeled!

By now, the chickpeas should be finished cooking. Wipe out the pan and put it back on the burner over medium heat. Heat about 1 tbsp oil, then add the kale and sauté for about 5 minutes. Store in a container by itself in the fridge.
Finally, your prepping is finished! Make sure each dish is stored safely in the fridge for prolonged freshness.

name: Miso-Ginger Chicken Thighs or Tofu
ingredients: Prepped miso-ginger chicken thighs or tofu
Vegetables to create a salad or sauté. Cabbage, broccoli, carrots, bell peppers, and mushrooms all pair well with the flavors in the miso-ginger sauce.
Optional: a whole grain- or seed-based source of carbohydrates, like brown rice, wild rice, quinoa, or even whole grain bread to make a sandwich.
steps: Reheat the miso-ginger tofu or chicken using your preferred method (the microwave or oven should work fine).
Prepare vegetables to eat along with your chicken or tofu. Make a salad or sauté- whatever your heart desires!

If you’d like, reheat or prepare a whole grain- or seed-based source of carbohydrates to include in your meal. You can also use whole grain bread to make a sandwich with your tofu or chicken, and enjoy a salad or sauté on the side.

name: Moroccan Spiced Chickpea Bowls
ingredients: ¼ cup finely chopped fresh mint leaves
steps: Make the yogurt sauce by combining all the ingredients in a medium bowl and whisking until combined. Season with salt and pepper to taste, and give it another stir.

Divide the brown rice between 4 bowls. Divide the chickpea mixture and sautéed kale over the brown rice and heat each bowl in the microwave. Alternatively you can mix all these ingredients in a saucepan and reheat on the stovetop. Once the reheated rice, chickpea mixture, and sautéed kale are heated and divided into serving bowls, add avocado slices and an egg to each bowl. Drizzle with the yogurt mint sauce. Enjoy!

name: Week 2 Meal Prep
ingredients: 1 cup skin-on raw almonds, coarsely chopped
⅓ cup dried apricots, coarsely chopped
4 tbsp unsalted butter, melted, or substitute 4 tbsp coconut oil
½ cup plus 2 tbsp honey, divided
steps: Make sure a rack is set in the middle of the oven, and then preheat the oven to 275°F. Prepare a baking sheet with parchment paper. Fill a pot with water and a few shakes of salt and set it on the stove over high heat to boil.

Start on the granola: Heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the millet and cook, stirring frequently, until toasted and golden-brown, 3 to 4 minutes. Remove from the heat and let cool for 5 minutes.

Once the pot of water is boiling, add the pasta and cook following the instructions on the box. Once you drain the pasta, lightly coat it in oil (to keep it from clumping together) and store it in an air-tight container in the fridge.

Finish making the granola: After 5 minutes of cooling, transfer the millet to a large bowl. Add the almonds, pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, apricots, chia seeds, flaxseed meal, sesame seeds, melted butter, and ½ cup of honey to the bowl and stir to combine. Pour the mixture onto the prepared baking sheet and spread into an even layer. Set it on the middle rack and bake for 20 minutes. After 20 minutes, stir the mixture and drizzle it with the remaining 2 tablespoons of honey. Let it bake for 10 minutes more, then transfer the baking sheet to a wire rack or countertop to cool. Once it’s completely cooled, store the granola in an airtight container in the fridge or pantry.

Make the marinated white beans: Chop the shallots and herbs, and then combine all the ingredients in a bowl. Give it a taste and season with more salt and pepper as needed. Store in the fridge. If you’d like, chop a little extra shallots and herbs and whisk them together with ¾ cup olive oil, ¼ cup balsamic vinegar, a tsp of honey, and a tbsp of dijon mustard for an easy salad dressing to use for your salads throughout the week.

Wash and prep the lettuce and cherry tomatoes. The tomatoes can be halved, and the lettuce can be chopped or ripped into big pieces. If you have other ingredients for salad, wash and cut them now. Store in the fridge. Finished!

name: Kielbasa Sauerkraut and Potato Skillet
ingredients: 2 14 oz packages of organic, antibiotic- and added hormone- free beef Kielbasa, sliced, or substitute vegan meat alternative
steps: In a large skillet heat the oil over medium heat. Add in the potatoes, carrots, and onions to the pan and cook until they have softened.
Stir in the garlic powder, peppers, and kielbasa, cook until the kielbasa is warmed through.
Add in the sauerkraut and cook for five minutes until the sauerkraut is heated through. Season with salt and pepper if needed.

name: Thai Meatball Khao Soi
ingredients: 2 green onions, finely chopped, plus more for serving
4 baby bok choy or 1 bunch kale, chopped
¼ cup Thai red curry paste
3 cups low-sodium chicken broth, or substitute vegetable broth
3 tablespoons fish sauce, or substitute coconut aminos
1 tablespoon honey, or substitute maple syrup
¼ cup each fresh cilantro and Thai or regular basil, chopped, plus more for serving
8 ounces gluten free egg or rice noodles, or substitute sweet potato noodles for a grain-free option
Sliced limes and thinly sliced shallots, for serving chili oil, for serving
steps: Add the chicken, green onions, ginger, garlic, and a pinch of pepper to a bowl. Mix until just combined. Coat your hands with a bit of oil, and roll the meat into tablespoon-size balls (will make 15-20 meatballs).

Heat a large pot over medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp oil. When the oil shimmers, add the meatballs and sear until crisp, about 4-5 minutes, turning them 2-3 times. Toss in the bok choy and cook 2 minutes more, until just charred on the edges. Transfer everything to a plate.

To the same pot, add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil, and the curry paste. Cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Stir in the coconut milk, chicken broth, fish sauce, and honey. Slide the meatballs and bok choy back into the soup. Simmer over medium heat until the meatballs are cooked through, 5-8 minutes. Stir in the herbs.
Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package directions.
Divide the noodles between bowls and ladle the soup over. Top each bowl as desired with basil, green onions, shallots, lime juice, and chili oil.

name: Creamy Tomato Mushroom Pasta for 2
ingredients: 250g gluten free pasta, or use a grain-free pasta like Banza chickpea pasta
steps: Cook the pasta according to the packaging instructions. Meanwhile, add the onion and mushrooms to a non-stick frying pan and cook for around 5 minutes, until the mushrooms soften.

Add the tomato purée, tamari, maple syrup, coconut milk, and season to taste with black pepper. Continue stirring for a further five minutes until the sauce thickens. At the last minute, stir in the cooked pasta together with the kale.

name: Garlic Roasted Chickpea Pasta
ingredients: ½ lb gluten free pasta (or use a grain free pasta for a paleo option)
1 tbsp Herb De Provence (or a pinch of as many of the following as you have in your kitchen: thyme, basil, rosemary, tarragon, savory, marjoram, oregano, and bay leaf)
Small handful fresh cilantro or rosemary
3 tbsp nutritional yeast (optional, but highly recommended due its high levels of B-1, B-2, B-6, B-12, and protein)
steps: Preheat the oven to 440F. On a baking sheet add the tomatoes and chickpeas. Drizzle over the olive oil then season with salt, pepper, Herbs de Provence or substitute, and garlic powder. Bake in the oven for 22 minutes, then bump the heat up to 475F and bake for another 7-10 minutes, until charred.

Meanwhile cook the pasta. While pasta is cooking add 1 ½ tbsp butter to a pan and saute minced garlic and thinly sliced onions for about 4-6 minutes on low heat. Once pasta is done, add it directly to the pan with the onions and garlic. You may add a tbsp of the hot pasta water too. Then add nutritional yeast, a drizzle of olive oil, salt and pepper to taste, and chopped fresh herbs. Toss.
Once tomatoes and chickpeas are out of the oven, add to pasta, toss again, then serve!

name: Miso-Ginger Chicken Thighs or Tofu.
ingredients: 2 pounds boneless skinless chicken thighs, or substitute one block extra-firm tofu
1 (2-inch) piece fresh ginger, peeled and chopped
1 tbsp soy sauce, or substitute coconut aminos
1 tbsp chili garlic sauce OR red chili paste
steps: Take the chicken thighs out of their packaging and pat dry.

In a food processor or a small chopper, whiz the rest of the ingredients until they form a saucy red paste. Pour this over the chicken in a large bowl or pan and mix well. Refrigerate the chicken overnight or bake immediately.

To bake, heat the oven to 425°F. Spread the chicken in a single layer on a large baking sheet or roasting pan. Bake for 30 minutes, flipping the chicken thighs over once halfway through the baking time. When the internal temperature of the chicken is between 160 and 165°F, take the pan out and let the chicken rest for 5 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Moroccan Spiced Chickpea Bowls.
ingredients: ¼ cup finely chopped fresh mint leaves
steps: Make the yogurt sauce. Place the ingredients in a medium bowl, season with salt and pepper, and whisk until combined.

Start the rice. Place the brown rice, 1½ cups of water, and a generous pinch of salt in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer until tender, about 40 minutes. Fluff with a fork.

While the rice is cooking, make the bowls. Heat 2 tablespoons of the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat until shimmering. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft and fragrant, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic, harissa, tomato paste, cumin, paprika, and cinnamon, season with salt and pepper, and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in the chickpeas and tomatoes with their juices. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.

name: Soft and Chewy Granola.
ingredients: 1 cup skin-on raw almonds, coarsely chopped
⅓ cup dried apricots, coarsely chopped
½ cup plus 2 tbsp honey, divided, or substitute maple syrup
steps: Arrange a rack in the middle of the oven and heat to 275°F. Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper.

Heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat until shimmering. Add the millet and cook, stirring frequently, until toasted and golden-brown, 3 to 4 minutes. Remove from the heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Transfer to a large bowl.

Add the almonds, pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, apricots, chia seeds, flaxseed meal, sesame seeds, melted butter, and ½ cup of the honey and stir to combine. Pour the mixture onto the prepared baking sheet and spread into an even layer.
Bake for 20 minutes. Stir the mixture, drizzle with the remaining 2 tablespoons honey, and bake for 10 minutes more.
Transfer the baking sheet to a wire rack and cool the granola completely. Store in an airtight container.

name: Pasta Salad.
ingredients: optional add-ins: feta cheese, mozzarella balls, jarred marinated or pickled veggies, bell peppers, scallions, basil, cucumber
optional dressing ingredients: red wine vinegar, extra-virgin olive oil, black pepper, salt, lemon juice or juice from a jar of marinated or pickled veggies
steps: Boil water in a large pot on the stove. Once the water is boiling, add the pasta and cook following the instructions on the box. Once the pasta is drained, lightly coat it in oil.

While the pasta is cooking, wash and prep the lettuce and cherry tomatoes. The tomatoes can be halved, and the lettuce can be chopped or ripped into pieces. If you want to add any other ingredients to your salad, prepare them now.
Combine pasta with the other ingredients. Toss together and enjoy!

name: Thick Banana-Nut Smoothie
ingredients: Frozen peeled banana (or other frozen fruit such as berries)
Your choice of nuts (walnuts, pecans, cashews) or 2 tbsp nut butter
1-3 cups full-fat milk, or substitute plant-based milk
steps: Combine all ingredients in a blender. If it doesn’t seem cold enough (or if your banana isn’t frozen), add a few ice cubes. Blend until smooth and enjoy!

name: Marinated White Beans.
ingredients: Finely grated zest of 1 medium lemon
½ tsp kosher salt, plus more as needed
2 (15-ounce) cans cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
⅓ cup chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves
¼ cup chopped fresh oregano leaves
steps: Chop the shallots and herbs, and then combine all the ingredients in a bowl.
Give it a taste and season with more salt and pepper as needed. Store in the fridge.

name: Chia Pudding Jars
ingredients: 1½ cup full-fat milk, or substitute plant-based milk
optional toppings: fresh or frozen fruit, nuts, seeds, gluten-free granola, cocoa nibs, nut butter, protein powder, cinnamon, coconut flakes
steps: In three small jars, distribute the chia seeds, milk, and sweetener evenly. Wait 5-10 minutes for the seeds to gel, and then stir again.
Top each jar with your desired toppings. Then seal the lids on each jar and set them in the fridge. Let them sit for at least 1 hour before eating.

name: One Pan Salmon and Vegetables
ingredients: 3 to 4 tbsp avocado oil, or substitute olive oil
steps: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.

Chop the vegetables and place them in a large casserole dish. Drizzle them liberally with avocado oil (about 3 tablespoons). Sprinkle with half of the sea salt, lemon pepper, dried parsley, and ground ginger (reserve the remaining half of the seasonings for the salmon). Use your hands to toss everything together until the vegetables are well coated in oil and seasonings.
Bake on the center rack of the preheated oven 25 minutes. Stir the vegetables well and continue baking another 10 minutes.
While the vegetables are baking, slice the salmon into portion size fillets (if they aren’t already). Coat with avocado oil and sprinkle with sea salt, lemon pepper, dried parsley, and ground ginger.

Remove vegetables from the oven after the 35 minutes is up and stir well. Make small wells in the vegetables to make room for the salmon. Place salmon fillets in the casserole dish with the vegetables and bake for 20 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and vegetables have reached desired doneness.
Serve with any additional sides, such as coconut rice, and enjoy.

name: Salmon and Rice Bowl
ingredients: 3 tbsp mayonnaise, or substitute vegan mayo
3 tbsp sriracha sauce or hot sauce
steps: Rinse and then cook 1 cup of brown rice according to the directions on the package.
While the rice is cooking, mix together the sriracha, coconut aminos, and mayo until smooth. Then slice the avocado and prepare any other desired toppings.
Reheat the salmon and then distribute it evenly onto 3 plates. Gently flake it with a fork. Add a serving of cooked rice on top of each serving of salmon.
Add sliced avocado and a heaping spoonful of kimchi, as well as any other desired toppings.
Mix together and enjoy!

name: Week 3 Meal Prep
ingredients: 3 to 4 tbsp avocado oil, or substitute olive oil
steps: Set the oven to preheat to 400 degrees F. Rinse 1 cup of brown rice under cool, running water. Using a medium saucepan on the stovetop, cook 1 cup of brown rice according to the directions on the package. When the rice is finished, let it cool for at least 15 minutes, and then transfer it to a storage container and place it in the fridge.

Wash and chop the veggies and place them in a large casserole dish. Drizzle them liberally with oil. Sprinkle them with half of the salt, lemon pepper, dried parsley, and ground ginger (save the other half of the seasoning for the salmon). Use your hands to toss everything together until the veggies are well coated in oil and seasonings. Bake on the center rack of the preheated oven for 25 minutes. Then stir them, and let them continue baking for another 10 minutes.

While the vegetables are baking, slice the salmon into portion size filets (if they aren’t already). Coat with oil and sprinkle with the remaining salt and seasonings. Then make the spicy mayo sauce for the Salmon and Rice Bowl: Mix the mayo, sriracha, and coconut aminos until smooth. Store in a jar or airtight container and set in the fridge.

After the vegetables have been in the oven for the full 35 minutes, remove the dish from the oven. Make small wells in the vegetables to make room for the salmon. Place the filets in with the veggies and return the casserole dish to the oven to make for 20 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through and vegetables have reached desired crispiness.

Assemble the chia pudding jars: In three small jars, distribute the chia seeds, milk, and sweetener evenly. Wait 5-10 minutes for the seeds to gel, and then stir again. Top each jar with your desired toppings. Then seal the lids on each jar and set them in the fridge.

When the salmon and veggies have baked for 20 minutes, remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for at least 20 minutes. Remove half of the salmon and store in a separate container in the fridge. Then cover or lid the casserole dish (or transfer the remaining salmon and veggies to a different container) and store in the fridge.

name: Spiced Kiwi Smoothie
ingredients: 1 tbsp cashew butter (or substitute a different nut butter)
steps: Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth :)

name: Hemp Heart Oatmeal
ingredients: 1/4 cup hemp seeds (or “hemp hearts” or “hulled hemp hearts”)
optional 1/2 tbsp monk fruit sweetener or other sweetener of choice
steps: In a small saucepan, stir all the dry ingredients together: hemp seeds, chia seeds, ground flaxseed, unsweetened shredded coconut, optional sweetener, and cinnamon.
Whisk in almond milk, vanilla, and almond butter.

Bring the saucepan to the stove over medium heat, constantly stir with a wooden spoon until it forms bubbles on the side of the saucepan and the oatmeal starts to thicken. Keep cooking/stirring for 3-4 minutes or until it reaches your favorite texture. The longer you cook it, the dryer it will be as moisture evaporates.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving bowl. Let sit for 2-3 minutes to steam out, then add toppings of your choice.

name: Shrimp and Grits
ingredients: 2 cups half and half, or substitute plant-based half and half
½ tsp salt, or to taste (always taste food)
2 tbsp unsalted butter, or substitute plant-based butter
steps: Cook grits according to package instructions using half and half or heavy whipped cream (or milk) and chicken stock in place of water.

Generously season your grits with ½ teaspoon of salt and about ¼ teaspoon of ground black pepper, or to please your taste. Always add less and taste it. You can always add more. Stir and cook grits at the low temperature until liquid is absorbed and becomes creamy and smooth.

Stir in 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter and set aside and keep warm. It takes usually around 5-7 minutes to finish instant grits at a lower temperature or about 20 to 25 minutes if you going to make stone-ground grits. Read instructions on the back of the package as it could be very helpful.

name: Classic Banana Berry Smoothie
steps: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.
Taste and adjust sweetness if needed with honey.
Enjoy the smoothie immediately :)

name: One-Pot Veggie Pasta
ingredients: 2 cups of chopped vegetables (our favorites are bell peppers, zucchini, onion, cherry tomatoes, chopped asparagus, and mushrooms)
Fresh herbs for garnish (such as basil or parsley)
steps: In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Add the chopped vegetables to the pot and cook until they start to soften, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the canned tomatoes and vegetable broth to the pot and stir well.
Break the spaghetti in half and add it to the pot, making sure it’s fully submerged in the liquid.
Bring the liquid to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let it cook for about 10-15 minutes, or until the pasta is cooked through and most of the liquid has been absorbed.
Season the pasta with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve hot.

name: Creamy Veggie One-Pot Pasta
ingredients: 1 cup of chopped vegetables (such as zucchini noodles, mushrooms, and cherry tomatoes)
1/2 cup of plain Greek yogurt, or substitute plant-based yogurt
1/4 cup of grated Parmesan cheese, or substitute plant-based cheese, or omit
Fresh herbs and pine nuts for garnish (such as parsley or basil)
steps: In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Chop the vegetables as the pot heats. Then, add the minced garlic and chopped onion and cook until fragrant and onion is translucent, about 1-2 minutes.
Add the rest of the chopped vegetables to the pot and cook until they start to soften, about 5-7 minutes.
Break the spaghetti in half and add it to the pot, followed by the vegetable broth. Stir well.
Bring the liquid to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let it cook for about 10-15 minutes, or until the pasta is cooked through and most of the liquid has been absorbed.
Add the Greek yogurt and Parmesan cheese to the pot and stir well, until the sauce is creamy.
Season the pasta with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve hot.

name: One Pot Creamy Pasta and Greens
ingredients: 5 cups whole milk, plus more as needed, or substitute plant-based milk
10 oz gluten-free ditalini, orzo or pearl couscous
½ cup finely grated Parmesan, or substitute plant-based cheese
Salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes
steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and shallot, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring, until softened, 2 to 4 minutes.

Add the 5 cups milk, spinach, lemon peel and 2 teaspoons salt. Cook, stirring occasionally and breaking up the frozen spinach with your spoon, until the spinach is softened and the milk is just simmering, 5 to 10 minutes.

Add the pasta and cook, stirring often and vigorously, until the pasta is al dente, 20 to 25 minutes, adjusting the heat as needed to maintain a gentle simmer. If it looks dry at any point, add more milk. The mixture will continue to thicken as it sits, so it’s ok if the sauce looks a little soupy at this point.
Once the pasta is cooked, turn off the heat, discard the lemon peels, and stir in the Parmesan until melted. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes, and serve with more Parmesan.

name: Pasta with Peas and Bacon
ingredients: 4 strips of turkey bacon, sliced into ½ inch pieces, or substitute plant-based bacon, or omit
2 ¼ cups chicken stock, or substitute veggie broth
1 cup whole fat plain yogurt, or substitute plant-based yogurt
Zest and juice from ½ lemon, about 2 tbsp
steps: Heat a medium-sized pot over medium-high heat. Add the bacon and cook until crispy, about 8 minutes. Remove the bacon from the pot. If there are more than 2 tablespoons of oil in the pot, remove the extra. If there is less, add a little olive oil.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until they are soft and begin to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken stock, thyme, and chili flakes to the pot and bring to a boil. Add the pasta, stir, then cover and cook for 10 minutes, stirring the pasta twice while it cooks.

After 10 minutes there should still be a little liquid left in the bottom of the pot. Add the peas and stir. Add the yogurt, lemon zest, and lemon juice and stir one more time. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

name: One Pot Leek, Mushroom, and Pea Pasta
ingredients: 4 sprays of olive oil cooking spray, or substitute regular olive oil
150 ml (about 0.6 cups) whole milk, or substitute unsweetened dairy-free milk
steps: Mist a large, deep, lidded pan with cooking spray and set over a medium heat. Add the leeks and garlic, stir and cook for 3-5 minutes, or until the leeks are tender. Add the wholewheat spaghetti, sliced mushrooms, dried mixed herbs, chilli flakes and the lemon juice and zest, followed by the unsweetened almond milk and vegetable stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook, covered, for 4-5 minutes, then remove the lid and continue cooking for 4-5 minutes until the pasta is cooked and the liquid reduced and thickened to a loose sauce.
Add the frozen garden peas to the pan and cook for a further 2-3 minutes. Season to taste and serve topped with fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves.

name: Greek Yogurt Mac and Cheese
ingredients: 2 tbsp unsalted butter, or substitute plant-based butter or 1 1/2 tbsp olive oil
2 tbsp gluten free flour (go for one that mimics the texture of all-purpose flour, like coconut flour, cassava flour, or tapioca starch)
2 cups whole fat milk, or substitute unsweetened plant-based milk
1 1/2 cups shredded sharp cheddar cheese, or substitute plant-based cheese
3/4 cup whole fat Greek yogurt, or substitute unsweetened plant-based yogurt
steps: Preheat oven to 450 degrees F. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Stir in the elbow macaroni. Boil until just tender (don’t overcook it!). Drain the pasta in a colander and spray cool water over it to keep it from sticking together.
In a large pot, melt butter over medium heat. Whisk in the flour, salt, and black pepper. Stir to form a thick paste.

Slowly whisk in the milk, ¼ cup at a time. Heat the milk mixture over medium heat for a few minutes, whisking frequently, until it thickens and begins to bubble around the edges. Do not let the sauce boil.

Whisk in grated cheddar cheese. Stir until cheese is melted and a thick sauce forms. Stir in the Greek yogurt until smooth. Stir the cooked macaroni into the sauce, making sure all the pasta is evenly coated by sauce. Pour macaroni with cheese sauce into a greased 2 qt gratin or 8x8 baking dish, spreading it out into an even layer and scraping all remaining sauce from the pot over the top of the pasta.

Mix together the breadcrumbs and smoked paprika and evenly sprinkle over the top of the pasta. Place in the oven and bake at 450 degrees F for 15 minutes until the top and breadcrumbs are golden brown to your liking. Serve hot. For a spicy twist, substitute cayenne pepper for black pepper.

name: Vegan Grilled Cheese
ingredients: 4 tsp agar powder, or substitute arrowroot flour, cornstarch, cassava flour, xanthan gum, or tapioca starch
1/4 cup cold water to mix with tapioca starch
7 tsp tapioca starch (this is the same as 2 tbsp + 1 tsp)
steps: Prepare cheese molds by spraying a glass bowl or container with spray oil or rub any neutral-flavored oil on the molds to prevent sticking. (Recipe will make about 2 cups of cheese).
Add a 13.5 oz can of coconut milk (or another cheese base), 1/4 cup of warm water, 2 tbsp of nutritional yeast, 4 tsp agar agar, 1/2 tsp garlic, and 1 1/4 tsp salt.
Heat the cheese sauce over medium heat until it begins to boil.
Turn down the heat until it is just barely bubbling and allow it to boil for 5 minutes while stirring frequently.
Add 2 tbsp plus 1 tsp (or 7 tsp.) tapioca starch to 1/4 cup of cold water and stir until it dissolves.

Add the starch and water mixture to the boiling cheese sauce and stir it in with a whisk and cook for an additional 1 minute. (Your cheese will become very thick and stretchy). You can put this hot cheese sauce in your sandwich immediately or let it cool and set and then slice or shred it in your sandwich. To let it set into hard cheese, pour into a glass container and allow to cool uncovered in the refrigerator for at least 3 hours before shredding or slicing. Keep refrigerated for up to 5 days.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
ingredients: 400 g (14 oz) chicken breast cut into bite size chunks
steps: Add 400 g chicken breast, 2 garlic clove, 3 tablespoons yogurt and 1 tablespoon Tikka seasoning to a bowl. Mix well, cover and put in the fridge to marinate for 30 minutes.
Put 1 onion, 3 garlic clove and 1 teaspoon fresh ginger into a food processor. Blend to form a smooth paste. Alternatively, you could use a pestle and mortar.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a pan and add the onion paste. Gently cook for 2 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons Tikka seasoning, 400 g chopped tomatoes, 80 ml chicken stock and 10 g fresh coriander (cilantro). Stir and bring to the boil. Reduce to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes.
Preheat your broiler and thread the marinated chicken onto skewers (pre-soaked if using wooden). Put the chicken skewers under the broiler and cook for 15 minutes, turning regularly.
Blend the sauce so it is smooth.
Add 4 tablespoons yogurt and 20 g ground almonds. Mix well and gently heat.
Once the chicken is cooked through, add to the sauce. Stir so the chicken is coated.
Serve over Rice with gluten free Naan bread, Mango chutney and Raita :)

name: Hardy and Healthy Beans on Toast
ingredients: 1 tbsp olive or avocado oil (or sub water if oil-free)
1 medium shallot, finely chopped (about 75 g or 1/2 cup), or sub 1/2 small white onion -1/4 tsp sea salt (plus more to taste)
1/4 tsp black pepper (plus more to taste)
2 tsp garlic powder (or sub 2 cloves fresh minced garlic)
2 (15-oz) cans white beans, drained and rinsed (we prefer cannellini or Great Northern, but navy beans are more traditional), or use about 3 cups cooked white beans made from 1 cup dry
steps: Heat a large pot over medium heat. Once hot, add olive oil (or avocado oil or water), shallot, salt, and pepper and sauté until translucent and tender — about 3-5 minutes.

Add garlic powder (or minced garlic) and stir. Then add all the remaining sauce ingredients to the pot: water, cider vinegar, tomato paste, yellow mustard, maple syrup, unsulphured molasses. Stir again to combine. Whisk if needed to break up any clumps.

Add the drained and rinsed beans and stir to coat. Turn the heat to medium-high and let the mixture reduce for 8-10 minutes (you should be able to hear and see the sauce bubbling). If the sauce starts to spit, turn the heat down slightly. Avoid covering the pot or the sauce won’t reduce quickly.

Stir often to avoid sticking. After 10 minutes the baked beans should be done and there will be some sauce coating the beans. Turn off the heat. Taste test and adjust, adding more salt for overall flavor, maple syrup for sweetness, apple cider vinegar for tang, or molasses for depth of flavor. Place a lid on the pot to keep the beans warm.
If serving with toast (we prefer hearty sourdough), put your bread in the toaster. Once golden brown, remove — usually within 3 minutes.
Once toasted, option to butter your toast with vegan butter or drizzle with olive oil. Then top with the piping hot beans.

Best when fresh and eaten with a knife and fork. The toast will turn a little wet where the sauce lands, and that’s how it’s typically eaten. Alternatively, serve on a baked potato. Optionally, garnish with arugula, roasted cherry tomatoes, or wilted spinach.

Store leftover beans covered in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days, or in the freezer for up to 1 month. Reheat in a saucepan over medium heat until hot (adding a bit of water to thin as needed if too thick).

name: Ramen
ingredients: 2 portions of fresh or packed gluten-free egg noodles
steps: Prepare boiling water, add some salt, and place the two portions of gluten-free noodles. Boil for 4-5 minutes.
Drain and put into 2 bowls.
In a pan, heat olive oil over medium to low heat. Sauté garlic until fragrant then add grated ginger and quartered mushroom.
After the mushroom gets wilted, add 4 cups of chicken broth, and let them boil.
Add the tamari sauce, sesame oil, salt, pepper, and scallion. And let them boil for another 5 minutes.
Test the taste. You can add more salt and pepper according to your liking.
Using a slotted spoon, take out the mushroom from the ramen soup.
Pour the ramen soup over the cooked noodles and add sliced mushroom from the ramen soup and all the toppings to it. Your homemade gluten-free ramen is ready to enjoy.

name: Raw Kale Salad with Cashew, Ginger and Chili Dressing
ingredients: 2 carrots, use a peeler to slice into ribbons lengthways or you can grate them
steps: Wash the kale then place in a medium sized bowl. Cover with the lemon juice and massage this into the kale leaves. Then leave this to sit for 10 minutes. The lemon will “cook” the kale.
Cut up the bok choy and carrots.
To make your dressing, place the nut butter, soya sauce and olive oil in a bowl and whisk until combined. Then add in the remaining ingredients and whisk again.
Place all the vegetables in the bowl with the kale, and stir in the dressing, making sure all the vegetables are evenly covered. Sprinkle some nuts and seeds on the top and serve!

name: Palak Paneer
ingredients: 225 grams paneer cut into cubes (or substitute with a hard variety of vegan or nut-milk cheese)
1 green chili or more to taste
2 tbsp olive oil (substitute butter or avocado oil)
½ cup water or as needed
¼ tsp red chili powder or to taste
steps: To a pot of boiling water, add a dash of salt and then spinach leaves. Boil for about 2 to 3 minutes or until wilted.
Remove spinach and put it into ice water.
In a blender, add the spinach and make a smooth paste. Set this aside.

Heat a pan on medium heat. Once hot, add the oil and then add cumin, green chili, chopped and chopped ginger. Saute for a few minutes until it starts changing color, then add the chopped onions. Cook the onion for 2 to 3 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add in the prepared spinach puree and mix. Also add in about ½ cup water.
Once the spinach is cooked, add the garam masala, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix and cook for 1 minute.
Add the paneer and mix. Let the curry simmer for 3 to 4 minutes. Serve and enjoy!

name: Broccoli Fritters
ingredients: 2 eggs Click here for egg replacements
Parmesan to taste (or substitute vegan cheese)
steps: Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl and then fry (like a pancake) in a pan with some olive oil.
Serve over a salad mixture.

name: Chicken Salad
ingredients: 4 medium sized boneless skinless chicken breast
1 cup mayonnaise (or substitute with soybean oil-free mayo option for paleo)
1 tbsp dried parsley or a small handful fresh parsley, chopped
steps: Place the chicken breasts in a large pot and cover with water, add salt. Bring to a boil, cook until chicken is cooked fully. Remove from heat, and allow to cool. Once cooled, chop chicken into small bite-sized pieces or shred the chicken.

Add chicken to a large mixing bowl, then add the rest of the ingredients, except for the pecans. Mix well to combine, then transfer to a bowl. Top with pecans and extra parsley. Refrigerate salad until it’s cool. Then serve and enjoy!

name: Easy Chili
ingredients: 1 pound of grass fed organic beef, or substitute a ground meat alternative like Beyond Ground Beef
2-3 cans of beans, drained (kidney beans, black beans, pinto beans, and navy beans are all great)
15 ounces of organic chicken or beef stock, or substitute vegetable broth
15 ounces of tomato sauce (try not to get a kind with added sugars)
Salt, pepper, cumin, chili powder, cayenne powder to use as seasoning
Your favorite type of cheese (or vegan cheese), full-fat yogurt (or vegan yogurt), or avocado for topping
steps: Add the olive oil to a large soup pot and place it over medium-high heat for two minutes. Add the onion and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the ground beef to the pot. Break it apart with a wooden spoon. Cook for 6-7 minutes, until the beef is browned, stirring occasionally.
Add in 1-2 tablespoons of chili powder, cumin, salt, and a dash of black pepper and cayenne powder. Stir until well-combined.
Add the broth, drained beans, and tomato sauce. Stir well.
Bring the liquid to a low boil. Then, reduce the heat (low to medium-low) to gently simmer the chili, uncovered, for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it rest for 5-10 minutes before serving. Top with cheese, full-fat yogurt, and/or avocado.

name: Black Bean Burgers and Cauliflower Mash
ingredients: 2 slices gluten or grain free bread (crumbled)
½ cup gluten free flour, or substitute with a grain-free flour like cassava, coconut, or almond flour
steps: In a large bowl, mash the beans until almost smooth with a potato masher or for. Stir well once the desired texture is achieved.
Add ½ tablespoon of oil to grease the bottom of a small frying pan. Add the onions and sauté them until they are soft and translucent, about 3 to 5 minutes.
Combine the sautéed onions into the mashed black beans. Add the crumbled bread slices, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt and pepper to taste. Mix well with a wooden spoon.

Once the mixture is well combined, add the flour a few tablespoons at a time. Your burger mixture will be very thick, so use your hands to work the flour in well. You’ll obtain a firm dough after 2 to 3 minutes of working the mixture.
Divide the black bean mixture into six individual patties, approximately 1/2-inch thick. Roll a handful into a ball, then gently flatten it.

Use the remaining 1 ½ tablespoons of olive oil to pan-fry the burgers over medium-low heat. Allow the patties to slightly firm-up and brown on each side, about 3 minutes. If your pan is too hot, the burgers will brown too quickly and not be heated through and cooked in the middle, so adjust the heat as needed.

name: Butternut Squash Chili
ingredients: 1 small butternut squash peeled and diced
540 mL black beans drained and rinsed
1596 mL diced tomatoes plus their juices.
2 tbsp chili powder or to taste
1 tsp dried oregano or to taste
¾ tsp salt or to taste
½ tsp pepper or to taste
1 tsp chili flakes or to taste
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil in a large pot over medium until the onion is translucent.
Add carrot and celery to the pot and sauté for another 3-5 minutes.
Add all remaining ingredients to the pot, then increase heat to high bring to a low boil. Reduce to low-medium heat to bring chili to a simmer.
Simmer with the lid over low heat for 30-45 minutes, or until butternut squash is fork-tender and flavors have developed. Serve and enjoy!

name: Warming Tofu Kale Hash
ingredients: 2 small sweet potatoes, chopped into large bites, skin on (or sub 1 large)
½ tsp each sea salt and black pepper
1 red onion sliced into wedge length wise (skin and tops removed)
2 tbsp fresh parsley (plus more for serving)
1 large bundle kale, stems removed and chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 400F, and press out excess moisture from tofu. Here is a link to a website that teaches how to press tofu.
In the meantime, season sweet potatoes with ½ tbsp oil, 1 tsp tandoori masala spice, coconut sugar, and a pinch each salt and pepper. Toss to coat.
Season onion with ½ tbsp oil, ¼ tsp tandoori spice, and a pinch each salt and pepper. Toss to coat.

Bake onions and potatoes for a total of 25-35 minutes, flipping once near the halfway point to ensure even cooking. You’ll know they’re done when the onions are brown and caramelized, and the sweet potatoes are fork tender. Remove from oven and set aside.
While vegetables are roasting, place pressed tofu in a bowl and use two forks to crumble into small pieces. Season with fresh parsley, turmeric, and a healthy pinch each salt and pepper. Set aside.

Once the potatoes and onions are almost done cooking, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. 7. Once hot, add 1/2 Tbsp oil, tofu, and 1 tsp tandoori masala spice. Sauté for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, to dry and brown the tofu. Then remove from skillet and set aside.

Add remaining ½ tbsp oil to the skillet and add kale. Season with a healthy pinch each salt and pepper, 1 tsp tandoori masala spice blend, and toss to coat. Sauté, stirring frequently, to brown and wilt the kale – about 3-4 minutes.
Push kale to one side of the pan and add tofu back in to warm. Turn off heat but keep over the burner.

To serve, divide the kale between 2 serving plates, top with roasted sweet potatoes and onion, then tofu. Sprinkle with remaining chopped parsley and enjoy. For extra flavor, season with hot sauce and serve with a generous spoonful of hummus (optional).

name: Coconut Curry Soup
ingredients: 1 knob of ginger, peeled and grated

Anything you have for fresh veggie toppings (green onions, cilantro, basil, cabbage, asparagus, baby kale, carrots, zucchini shreds, bell peppers, mushrooms, spinach, broccoli, snap peas, bean sprouts)
steps: Press the tofu to drain the liquid.

Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger, and stir fry for a few minutes until fragrant. Don’t let the garlic burn or brown. Add the curry paste, turmeric, salt, and sugar. Stir fry for another few minutes. Add the potatoes, coconut milk, and broth. Simmer until the potatoes are soft.

Allow to cool slightly, then transfer to a blender and puree until smooth and silky. The soup should be rich and thick so that it coats the back of a spoon. Add in more water or broth ( about 2-3 cups) to thin out the soup as needed.

Remove the tofu from the press and cut into small pieces. In the same pot, heat a little more oil and add the tofu, stir frying until light golden brown. Pour in a small amount of the pureed soup – it will sizzle and pop, and it will also give the tofu the most wonderful color and caramelized exterior. Remove from heat with the tofu is deep golden brown and crispy.
Top each serving of soup with fresh veggie toppings and crispy caramelized tofu and enjoy your luscious salad-meets-soup combo :)

name: Green Power Protein Salad
ingredients: 1 small - medium head of broccoli chopped
2 kale leaves stems removed, torn into small pieces
1 cup of lentils cooked (lentils can be cooked in advance)
1 cup of cottage cheese (or substitute vegan cheese)
For the dressing: 2 tbsp olive oil 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 minced garlic clove, salt and pepper to taste
steps: Mix all salad ingredients together in a large bowl.
Whisk dressing ingredients together in a small bowl and drizzle over salad.
Enjoy!

name: Curry Veggie and Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup wild rice, rinsed, or substitute quinoa
1 bundle of asparagus, ends trimmed and chopped
A few tablespoons of olive oil or substitute coconut oil
1 bundle of kale, destemmed and shredded
steps: Place the rice in a saucepan and add 4 cups of water, along with a dash of salt. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a simmer (low to medium-low heat) and cover the pan. Cook at a simmer for 45 minutes. It should be chewy and some of the grains will have burst open. It may need an additional 10 to 15 minutes– keep checking the rice and stop cooking when the grains are tender. When it’s done, drain it, fluff it, and set aside.
Set the oven to 400 degrees and drizzle two sheet pans with olive oil.

Place beets, carrots, and yams into a large mixing bowl and drizzle with olive oil. Sprinkle curry powder and paprika over the veggies and stir together. Lay these veggies across one of the sheet pans and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes.

Toss the broccoli and asparagus in the same mixing bowl and soak up the extra oil and spices remaining in the bowl. Then, add these veggies to the second sheet pan and roast them for about 10-15 minutes.
In a large salad bowl, toss the kale in lemon juice and/or olive oil. Add the rice and roasted veggies. Toss together and enjoy!

name: Creamy Cocoa Coconut Shake
ingredients: 4 oz full fat coconut cream or milk – To make EXTRA creamy, freeze your coconut cream beforehand.
2 tbsp coconut MCT oil or melted coconut oil (grass fed butter also works)
8 oz unsweetened nut or oat drinking milk
½ tbsp creamy almond butter or other nut butter
Optional add-ins for sweetness: berries, cinnamon, banana, honey or maple syrup
steps: Place all ingredients into a blender, blend all together and go. If you add extra coconut oil, be sure to consume right away so the shake doesn’t get clumpy.

name: Green Goddess Fig Nourish Bowl
ingredients: 6 tbsp Vegan Green Goddess Dressing (2 tbsp per bowl), or other dressing of choice
1 Granny Smith or other green apple
3 tbsp Almonds and/or Seeds of choice
⅓ cup Italian Parsley (2 – 3 tbsp per bowl)
Sea Salt and Black Pepper, to taste
steps: Make the Vegan Green Goddess Dressing, if using.
Spiralize or thinly slice the apple; set aside.
Clean, dry, and slice or chop the greens and cabbage; set aside.
Slice figs in half; set aside.
Arrange the spinach and cabbage into serving bowls.
Divide the microgreens or sprouts, parsley, and apple and place around the spinach/cabbage in each bowl.
Add figs in 2-3 portions. Place on top of each bowl along with the almonds and seeds.
If using, add fresh berries, then drizzle balsamic vinegar over top of salad.
Cut lemon into wedges and add one to each salad to squeeze for fresh juice. Add salt and pepper to taste.

name: Drunken Chicken Zoodle Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb boneless chicken thighs or breast (the darker meat works best)
steps: Preheat oven to 350 F and oil a medium casserole dish. Set aside.
Clean your chicken then dice your chicken meat into small slices/cubes. Set aside.

Slice veggies and spiralize your zucchini. If you don’t have a spiralizer, just cut julienne style. Make sure to pat your zucchini noodles extra dry with a towel to remove excess water. Place zucchini noodles (zoodles) at the bottom of the casserole dish and set aside.

Next, mix your stir fry sauce. Whisk together the fish sauce, chili sauce, ginger, tamari, and sugar (to equal the dark soy sauce), lime, pinch of pepper. Taste and adjust with more tamari/sugar if needed. For Paleo option, use palm sugar or molasses.
Place meat, garlic, onion, oil in skillet or large pan. Sauté 5 minutes.

Add your stir fry veggie mix, sliced red pepper, sauce, and 2-3 tbsp tapioca starch. Stir fry 10-15 minutes until chicken no long pink (but not overcooked) and starch is mixed well with the meat/vegetables.
Add the Chicken stir fry with sauce on top of zoodles in the casserole dish; evenly.
Next whisk your eggs and coconut milk. Pour over casserole dish.
Bake 25-30 minutes or until the edges are golden brown and the egg mix is cooked through.
Remove from oven and garnish with Thai basil and optional sliced Thai peppers.
Salt/pepper to taste. Sesame seeds to sprinkle over top, if desired.

name: Easy Drunken Zoodle Chicken Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb boneless chicken thighs or breast (the darker meat works best)
steps: Preheat oven to 350 F and oil a medium casserole dish. Set aside.
Clean your chicken then dice your chicken meat into small slices/cubes. Set aside.

Slice veggies and spiralize your zucchini. If you don’t have a spiralizer, just cut julienne style. I use this spiralizer. Make sure to pat your zucchini noodles extra dry with a towel to remove excess water. Place zucchini noodles (zoodles) at the bottom of the casserole dish and set aside.

Next mix your stir fry sauce. Whisk together the fish sauce, chili sauce, ginger, tamari, sugar, lime, pinch of pepper. Taste and adjust with more tamari/sugar if needed. For Paleo option, use palm sugar or molasses.
Place meat, garlic, onion, oil in skillet or large pan. Sauté 5 minutes.

Add your stir fry veggie mix, sliced red pepper, sauce, and 2-3 tbsp tapioca starch. Stir fry 10-15 minutes until chicken no long pink (but not overcooked) and starch is mixed well with the meat/vegetables.
Add the Chicken stir fry with sauce on top of zoodles in the casserole dish; evenly.
Next whisk your eggs and coconut milk. Pour over casserole dish.
Bake 25-30 minutes or until the edges are golden brown and the egg mix is cooked through.
Remove from oven and garnish with Thai basil and optional sliced Thai peppers.
Salt/pepper to taste. Sesame seeds to sprinkle over top, if desired.

name: Roasted Sweet Potato Toast
ingredients: Nut butter of your choice (I recommend cashew butter or almond butter)
Pumpkin seeds or another variety of seeds for topping
steps: Preheat oven to 400F.
Place halved sweet potato(es) –orange part facing up– on a baking sheet. Drizzle olive oil over them, then give each half a sprinkle of salt. Place in the preheated oven and roast for 25-30 mins.
Spread nut butter over your halved sweet potato. Top with cinnamon and pumpkin seeds. Enjoy!

name: Veggie Loaded Fried Rice
ingredients: 4 tbsp avocado oil or safflower oil, divided
3 large eggs, whisked together Click here for egg replacements
1½ small white onion, finely chopped (about 1 cup)
3 large carrots, finely chopped (about ½ cup)
3½ cups additional veggies, cut into very small pieces for quick cooking (options include snow peas, asparagus, broccoli, cabbage, bell pepper, and/or fresh or frozen peas—no need to thaw first)
½ teaspoon salt, more to taste
½ tablespoon grated or finely minced fresh ginger
3½ large cloves garlic, pressed or minced
3 cups + 3½ tbsp cooked brown rice, or substitute quinoa
1½ cup greens (optional), such as spinach, baby kale or tatsoi
1½ tablespoon reduced-sodium tamari or coconut aminos
Chili-garlic sauce or sriracha, for serving (optional)
steps: This recipe comes together quickly. Before you get started, make sure that all of your ingredients are prepped and within an arm’s reach from the stove. Also have an empty bowl nearby for holding the cooked eggs and veggies. I’m suggesting that you start over medium-high heat, but if at any point you catch a whiff of oil or food burning, reduce the heat to medium.

Warm a large cast iron or stainless steel skillet over medium-high heat until a few drops of water evaporate within a couple of seconds. Immediately add 1 ½ teaspoons of oil and swirl the pan to coat the bottom. Add the scrambled eggs and swirl the pan so they cover the bottom. Cook until they are just lightly set, flipping or stirring along the way. Transfer the eggs to a bowl and wipe out the pan with a heat-proof spatula.
Return the pan to heat and add 1 tablespoon of oil. Add the onion and carrots and cook, stirring often, until the onions are translucent and the carrots are tender, about 3 to 5 minutes.

Add the remaining veggies and salt. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally (don’t stir too often, or the veggies won’t have a chance to turn golden on the edges), until the veggies are cooked through and turning golden, about 3 to 5 more minutes. In the meantime, use the edge of your spatula or a spoon to break up the scrambled eggs into smaller pieces.

Use a big spatula or spoon to transfer the contents of the pan to the bowl with the cooked eggs. Return the pan to heat and the remaining 1 tablespoon oil. Add the ginger, garlic and red pepper flakes, and cook until fragrant while stirring constantly, about 30 seconds. Add the rice and mix it all together. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the rice is hot and starting to turn golden on the edges, about 3 to 5 minutes.

Add the greens (if using) and green onions, and stir to combine. Add the cooked veggies and eggs and stir to combine. Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the tamari and sesame oil. Taste, and add a little more tamari if you’d like more soy flavor (don’t overdo it or it will drown out the other flavors) or salt, if the dish needs an extra boost of overall flavor.

Divide into bowls and serve immediately. I usually serve mine with chili-garlic sauce or sriracha on the side. Leftovers store well in the refrigerator, covered, for 3 to 4 days (if you used purple cabbage, it might stain your scrambled eggs a funny blue color, but it’s fine to eat).

name: Smashed Sweet Potato Breakfast Bowl
ingredients: ¼-½ cup milk or unsweetened vanilla non-dairy milk (I used almond)
2 tbsp unsalted almond butter (or substitute coconut oil)
optional toppings: full fat yogurt, granola, fruit, cinnamon, nut butter, protein powder
steps: Preheat the oven to 400F.
Wash the potatoes using a veggie brush and add to a baking tray lined with parchment paper, a silicone baking mat, or aluminum foil.
Bake for about 45-60 minutes or until easily pierced with a fork.
Let the potatoes cool, then carefully remove the skin and add the flesh to a large mixing bowl.
Add all of the remaining ingredients and beat with an electric mixer until nice and fluffy. Keep as is or heat up on the stove if the potatoes were previously refrigerated.
Transfer to bowls and serve with fruit or other toppings.

name: Microgreens Salad
ingredients: Organic microgreens (ex sunflower, basil, radish, amaranth, arugula)
steps: Combine all of the microgreens and salad ingredients in a bowl.
Whisk up the dressing ingredients in a separate bowl or blender and combine well with the salad immediately before serving.

name: Loaded Burger Bowl (Vegetarian option)
ingredients: Vegetarian: 1 pound of “Beyond Beef” or “Impossible Burger” or whatever is your favorite beef substitute.
steps: Heat a skillet over medium high heat. Add the protein and spices. Brown the protein or alternative, breaking it up as it cooks, until it is fully cooked through.
While the protein is browning, make the special sauce. Add all of the ingredients to a small bowl and whisk together until well combined.
Assemble your burger bowls. Start with romaine lettuce then add the toppings and cooked ground beef. Drizzle the special sauce over everything and then enjoy!

name: Shakshuka
ingredients: 1 can (28 ounces) diced tomatoes (no salt added)
4 large eggs Click here for egg replacements
steps: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add olive oil, onion, and bell peppers. Cook 5 to 7 minutes, or until softened. Add the minced garlic, black pepper, Italian season, and kosher salt. Stir and cook for 2 to 3 minutes, then add tomatoes. Turn heat to medium, cover, and let cook for 5 minutes.

Remove lid and create four small holes in the tomato mixture. Crack an egg into each hole, then cover and cook for an additional 6 minutes, until white is firm and yolk is set but still able to be punctured with fork. (If you prefer a set egg with a firm yolk, cook for 8 minutes.) 3. 3. Remove from heat and serve with a potato or toasted bread.

name: Teriyaki Chicken Lettuce Wraps
ingredients: 1 pound boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
steps: Add the coconut oil to a pan or skillet and warm on a medium high heat. When the oil is melted and hot add the chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside for a few minutes as you make the sauce
Add the chilli, garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for 1 minutes, stirring constantly.

Add the honey and coconut aminos. Stir well and bring the sauce to a boil and then reduce the heat down to medium-low or until it’s only simmering and let cook for about 5 more minutes. Stir frequently to prevent burning. The sauce should reduce down and turn into a thick glaze. Add the chicken back to the pan and toss. Cook for another minute with the chicken in the sauce then remove from the heat.
Add a few spoonfuls of the chicken mixture into each lettuce cup and top with shredded carrot, green onions, sesame seeds and peanuts/cashews and serve.

name: Maghmour
ingredients: ½ cup chopped cilantro fresh only
2 medium sized eggplant or 1 large cubed and fried
steps: In a skillet, heat the olive oil and caramelized the sliced onions.
Add the tomatoes, cilantro and garlic. Sauté for a few minute and then cover and simmer for about 5 minutes on medium-low heat.
Add in the tomato paste, tomato puree sauce, salt, spice and water. Mix well and cover to simmer for another 5 minutes.
Remove the tomato sauce from the heat and pour into a baking dish. Place the remainder of the ingredients on top of the sauce, spreading them around evenly as shown in the photo.
Cover with aluminum foil and bake the dish in a preheated 375 degree oven for 30 minutes.
Remove and serve if you prefer eating it hot or keep on room temperature to cool down before placing in the fridge for extra cooling.
You can store in an air tight container after serving for up to 3 days.

name: Banana Walnut Pancakes
ingredients: 1 egg Click here for egg replacements
1 tbsp melted butter, ghee or coconut oil
½ cup sorghum, amaranth, teff, oat, chickpea, brown rice, or a different kind of gluten-free flour
steps: Mash banana in a large bowl. Add baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, salt, vanilla and egg; whisk until just combined.
Add melted butter and ¼ cup almond milk; whisk everything together. Add flour, and stir until combined.
Add more almond milk if batter is too thick.

Spray a griddle or skillet with non-stick spray or coat with melted butter, and then heat to med-low. Once your griddle/skillet is warm, pour a little less than ¼ cup batter onto pan; add walnuts and turn heat down to low.
Cook for 3 minutes or until the batter starts to bubble on top. Flip pancake and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes. Continue with remainder of batter.
Top as desired and serve.

name: Savory Oatmeal with Eggs and Avocado
ingredients: 1 and 1/2 cups McCann’s Steel Cut Irish Oatmeal
steps: Heat the onion, mushrooms, and garlic in a large pot with the olive oil over medium-high heat. Cook for 6-7 minutes, until the onions are slightly browned and the mushrooms are cooked down.
Add the oatmeal and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer 25-30 minutes, until the oatmeal is tender and the mixture has thickened. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Top with fried eggs, avocado, arugula, parmesan, parsley, and sautéed mushrooms.

name: Mujadara (Lentils and Rice with Caramelized Onions)
ingredients: 4 medium cloves garlic, smashed and peeled
1 ¾ teaspoons fine sea salt, divided
1 cup brown rice (regular, not quick-cooking), rinsed and drained
1 cup regular brown or green lentils, picked over for debris, rinsed and drained
2 medium-to-large yellow onions, halved and thinly sliced
½ cup thinly sliced green onions (from 1 bunch), divided
½ cup chopped fresh cilantro or flat-leaf parsley, divided
Plain whole-milk or Greek yogurt, for serving (or substitute nut-milk or dairy free yogurt)
Spicy sauce, for serving (optional): shatta or zhoug or store-bought chili-garlic sauce or even sriracha
steps: In a large Dutch oven or soup pot, combine the garlic, bay leaves, cumin, 1 ½ teaspoons of the salt and about 20 twists of freshly ground black pepper. Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat.
Once boiling, stir in the rice and reduce the heat to medium. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally and adjusting the heat as necessary to maintain a controlled simmer, for 10 minutes.

Stir in the lentils and let the mixture return to a simmer. 4. Cover again, reduce the heat to medium-low, and cook until the liquid is absorbed and the rice and lentils are tender, about 20 to 23 minutes. 5.Meanwhile, warm the olive oil in a large (12-inch) skillet over medium-high heat. When it’s warm enough that a slice of onion sizzles on contact, add the remaining onions. Stir to combine.

Stir only every 3 minutes or so at first, then more often once the onions at the edges of the pan start browning. If the onions are browning before they have softened, dial down the heat to give them more time. Cook until the onions are deeply caramelized and starting to crisp at the edges, about 20 to 30 minutes. In the meantime, line a large plate or cutting board with a couple paper towels. Using a slotted spoon or fish spatula, transfer the onions to the lined plate and spread them evenly across. Sprinkle the remaining ¼ teaspoon salt over the onions. They’ll crisp up as they cool.

When the lentils and rice are done cooking, drain off any excess water (if there is any) and return the mixture to the pot, off the heat. Lay a kitchen towel across the top of the pot to absorb steam, then cover the pot and let it rest for 10 minutes.

Remove the lid, discard the bay leaves, and smash the garlic cloves against the side of the pan with a fork. Add about ¾ths of the green onions and cilantro, reserving the rest for garnish. Gently stir and fluff the rice with a fork. Season to taste with additional salt and pepper, if necessary.

Transfer the rice and lentil mixture to a large serving platter or bowl. Top with the caramelized onions and the remaining green onions and cilantro. Serve hot, warm or at room temperature, with yogurt and spicy sauce (optional) on the side.

name: Quick Carrot Rice Breakfast Nasi Goreng
ingredients: 3 tbsp tamari sauce (gluten free) or coconut aminos for paleo option
steps: Boil the tamari, molasses, and water in a saucepan over high heat. Then reduce heat to medium-low and cook until molasses has dissolved with the tamari. Place in separate bowl and set aside.
You will have extra sweet soy sauce. Store this for later!

name: Kale and Chickpea Stew
ingredients: 1 x 400g tin chickpeas, drained and rinsed
2 tsp fresh thyme (or 1 tsp dried thyme)
2 large handfuls of kale, de-stemmed and torn into bite-sized pieces
Top with parsley and parmesan cheese if you’d like
steps: In a large pot, heat some extra virgin olive oil over medium heat. Add the shallots and let cook until tender. Then add the garlic, thyme, and salt and pepper to taste. Cook until fragrant.

Next, pour in the chickpeas and let cook one to two minutes. Then add the tomatoes, vegetable broth, and parmesan rind. Push the tomatoes to the side of the pan to break them down. Bring everything to a boil and then simmer for around 20 minutes

A few minutes before you would like to serve add in the chopped kale and allow to wilt. Try and keep the kale bring green if possible. Top with parsley and some grated parmesan and serve with a thick, crusty slice of gluten free bread. Enjoy!

name: One Pan Lemon Chicken with Asparagus
ingredients: ¼ cup gluten free flour, or substitute tapioca flour
¾ tsp sea salt, plus more for seasoning
½ tsp ground black pepper, plus more for seasoning
1 pound asparagus stalks, ends trimmed and then cut in half
1 cup chicken stock, aim for a lower sodium stock
1 tbsp fresh parsley, roughly chopped + more for garnishing
steps: Place the chicken breasts between two pieces of plastic cling wrap and pound them down to make them even in thickness. This will help the chicken cook evenly and make for more tender chicken. If your breasts are extra thick you can also just cut/slice them in half.
Place the flour, salt & pepper in a shallow dish and gently toss the chicken breasts to coat in flour.

In a large skillet add 1 tablespoon of olive oil and bring to a medium high heat. When the oil is hot add the chicken to the skillet and cook each side for about 5 minutes or until golden and cooked through. Once cooked remove the chicken and place on a paper towel lined plate. Set aside while you cook the asparagus.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in the skillet. Add the asparagus stalks and sauté for a minute. Add the garlic and sauté another minute longer until fragrant.

In a small bowl or cup whisk together the lemon juice and mustard until fully mixed. Pour into the skillet with the asparagus along with the chicken stock and the zest. Bring the liquid to a boil and then reduce down to a simmer. Cover and let cook another 3-4 minutes or until the asparagus is tender.
Stir in the parsley and then add the chicken back to the pan and rotate the breasts to coat in the liquids. Taste the sauce and season with more salt & pepper as needed.

name: Guacamole Chicken Salad
ingredients: 1-2 jalapeno pepper minced (adjust for spice preference)
2-3 Tbsp fresh lime juice or to taste
2 Tbsp chopped fresh cilantro plus more for garnish
1 lb chicken breast seasoned with salt and pepper and cooked (about 2-2.5 cups cooked)
steps: Cook chicken (this step should be done ahead of time so the chicken has time to cool.) I recommend poaching the chicken. Here is a link to instructions on how to poach chicken.

Preheat your oven to 400 degrees and line a baking sheet with foil. Drizzle olive oil over chicken and turn to coat. Season on both sides with sea salt, black pepper, plus onion + garlic powder, if desired. Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes or until cooked through (no longer pink in the middle and juices run clear.)
Allow chicken to cool completely, then either dice or shred depending on what you prefer for chicken salad.

In a large bowl, add the cooked diced chicken with all remaining ingredients except for the salt. Mix well, mashing avocado as you mix. Once fully combined, add sea salt to taste. Serve immediately, leftovers will keep for about a day in the refrigerator but because the avocado will brown, it won’t last much longer than that. Enjoy!

name: Easy Shrimp Stirfry
ingredients: 1 tbsp Avocado Oil or oil of choice
1 ½ lb Shrimp raw, peeled, deveined
¼ tsp Crushed Red Pepper Flakes
steps: Toss the shrimp in the salt and pepper flakes, then heat the avocado oil in a large skillet over medium/high heat. Add in the seasoned shrimp and cook until light pink in color (3-5 minutes) and set aside.

Add in the sesame oil and all of the veggies (except ginger and garlic) cook for 5-7 minutes until the veggies are a little tender but still have bite to them. Then add in the ginger, garlic, rice vinegar and coconut aminos. Let cook another 2-3 minutes and stir in the shrimp. Season with salt if needed.
Remove from heat, top with sesame seeds and green onions.

name: Cauliflower Popcorn
ingredients: ½ head of cauliflower, diced small like popcorn
¼ cup extra virgin olive oil
steps: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F.
Toss the cut up cauliflower in olive oil.
Sprinkle salt all over the cauliflower. Spread on a baking sheet with parchment paper. Bake for 30 minutes, flipping the cauliflower at least twice. The cauliflower will burn slightly.
Take out of the oven and sprinkle onion powder and chives over all the pieces or your preferred popcorn flavoring.

name: Chocolate Hazelnut Protein Cookies
ingredients: 8 ounce hazelnuts, or substitute other nut like cashews, almonds, walnuts, pecans, or peanuts
½ cup dark dairy free chocolate chips (I recommend the Enjoy Life brand)
½ cup 1-1 Sugar Replacement Monk Fruit Powder
½ cup chocolate protein powder (if you don’t have protein power, skip this or replace with a pseudo flour like buckwheat flour, almond flour, coconut flour, or flax meal)
Extra dark chocolate (melted) and hazelnuts (or other nut) for topping, substitute dairy-free chocolate
steps: First blend your hazelnuts and chocolate chips together in a food processor. Make a “mealy” base. It will be moist from the chocolate.
Place in bowl.
Add in your banana and coconut oil and extracts. Beat until blended.
Add in your sugar, protein powder, extract, and cocoa. Mix again until blended.
Roll dough into golf size balls and place on greased baking sheet. Press flat with hand or back of spoon.
Bake at 350F for 10-12 minutes or until cookies are browned a bit on the corners. They will still be soft but will harden later in fridge.
While cookies or cooling, melt extra dark chocolate and drizzle the dark chocolate on top.Then crush extra hazelnut (you can crush in ziplock bag or food processor really quick) and sprinkle on top.
Place cookies in fridge for 30 minutes so harden
Makes 20-22 protein cookies.

name: Veggie Buns
steps: To make the dough, mix flour & salt in a large bowl until well combined. Make a well in the middle & pour in hot water. Using a spatula, fold in all flour until a shaggy dough forms with no dry spots of flour in the bowl (if it does, add cold water, 1 tablespoon at a time). Transfer flour to a cleaned surface & knead until a smooth dough forms, about 10mins. Cover with a damp towel & rest for 30 mins.⁣

Meanwhile, place tofu in a cheesecloth bag & wring out all the liquid. Saute chopped green beans with 1 teaspoon oil in a pan for about 1-2 mins. Add green beans into a bowl along with tofu, carrot & seasoning ingredients. Mix well & divide the mixture into 10 portions & form it into a patty-like shape.⁣
When ready, dust flour on the surface, roll dough into a log & divide into 10 equal balls.⁣

To assemble, take one dough, flatten it with your palm & roll into a wrapper, about 4.5 inches diameter by keeping the middle part thicker Place one filling patty in the middle & pleat to seal. Dab the smooth side with water then gently press in some sesame seeds.⁣

To cook the buns, heat a non-stick pan (used 10″) with 3 tsp oil. Slightly flatten each bun with your palms & place them in the pan. Fit as many as you can in one layer. Cook until the bottom turns golden brown, flip & add 1 to 1.5 cups of hot water (be sure water covers > than ⅓ of the bun’s height). Cover with a lid, preferably glass & turn heat to low-med. Cook until all water has been absorbed, about 3-5mins. Alternatively, preheat oven to 375F (195°C) & brush a little oil on the side of the buns & bake for about 20-25 mins or until golden brown, flipping in between.
Serve with chopped green onions & soy sauce or coconut aminos.

name: Strawberry Banana Baked Oatmeal Bites with Chocolate Chips
ingredients: 3 cups old fashioned oats (use certified gluten-free oats to make this recipe gluten free)
steps: Preheat oven 350 degrees F.
Lightly spray a muffin pan with oil (I used coconut oil).
In a medium bowl, whisk together the smashed bananas, milk and vanilla.
In another bowl, combine the oats, flax, baking powder, salt and cinnamon.
Pour the dry ingredients into the wet and stir until well combined.
Stir in the strawberries and chocolate chips (if using).
Fill the muffin tins to the top with the oatmeal mixture and press down gently to make them compact.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until the edges are golden brown and the oatmeal is baked through.
Let cool in the pan on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes.This will ensure that the oatmeal bites settle and hold together.
Remove muffins from pan and enjoy! Store any leftovers in the fridge.

name: Tex Mex Sweet Potato Hash
ingredients: 1 lb ground beef or ground turkey (grass fed, hormone and antibiotic free), or substitute Beyond Meat or meat other meat alternative
Taco seasoning (choose one with no added starch and low sugar)
One medium to large sweet potato, peeled and cut into small cubes equaling about 2½ – 3 cups.
1-2 large handfuls of spinach, kale or any other dark leafy green
