Optional Toppings: Fresh cilantro, green onions, avocado, guacamole, jalapeños, fried egg, sour cream, Mexican blend cheese or cotija cheese
steps: Place a skillet over medium-high heat. Add beef, seasonings and water to the pan. Cook until beef is browned. Remove beef from pan, set aside. Skip this step if using leftover taco meat.
In the same pan the beef was browned in, add ½ tablespoon of oil and heat over medium until melted and hot.
Add the diced sweet potatoes, dash with salt and pepper, and sauté for about 10-15 minutes or until just before fully cooked through, stirring occasionally. Add more oil as needed.
Add remaining ½ tablespoon of oil as well as the onions, red pepper, and mushroom and sauté for about 3 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Fold in spinach and taco meat and continue to cook until heated through and spinach is wilted.

name: Quinoa Salad with Orange Vinaigrette and Arugula
ingredients: 1 ½ cups low sodium vegetable broth
⅓ cup sliced almonds, or substitute a different type of nut
⅓ cup crumbled feta (or substitute vegan cheese)
1 avocado, diced (Optional if you like Avocado)
steps: Combine quinoa, 1 ½ cups orange juice, and vegetable broth in a medium pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until quinoa is tender. Allow to cool (this step can be done in advance).
Place cooked quinoa, white beans, arugula, almonds, and feta in a large bowl.
In a small jar, combine all vinaigrette ingredients and shake to combine. Pour vinaigrette over quinoa mixture and toss to coat.
Top salad with diced avocado and additional feta and almonds, if desired.

name: Shrimp Lettuce Wraps with Sriracha Dipping Sauce
ingredients: 1 zucchini, finely chopped, julienned, or spiralized
½ red bell pepper, thinly sliced
steps: Heat the sesame oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the green onion, ginger, zucchini, red pepper, and mushrooms. Cook for 3-4 minutes, until fragrant and softened.
Add the shrimp, soy sauce, and lime juice. Cook another 3-4 minutes, until the shrimp is pink and cooked through and the liquid has mostly evaporated.
Meanwhile, stir together all the sauce ingredients in a medium bowl.
Spoon the shrimp mixture into lettuce cups, garnish with the cilantro, green onions, and lime, and serve with the dipping sauce.

name: Peanut Butter Jam Porridge
ingredients: ½ cup mixture of any nuts, seeds or coconut
1 tsp any light-colored sweetener, such as rice malt syrup or maple syrup
steps: Preheat oven to 180C.
For the granola: Mix all ingredients in a bowl and spread on a lined baking sheet. Bake for 10 minutes or until golden brown.
Meanwhile, for the raspberry chia jam: Mash the raspberries with a fork and mix through chia seeds. Set aside.

Meanwhile, for the porridge: Add all ingredients to a small saucepan over high heat. Bring to a boil for 2 minutes and reduce to a simmer. Stir porridge to make sure it doesn’t stick to the bottom of the saucepan. When the porridge has reached your desired consistency, separate into 2 bowls.
Decorate the 2 bowls with granola, chia jam, additional peanut butter and any other toppings as desired. Enjoy!

name: Spicy Chickpea Power Bowl with Tzatsiki
ingredients: 1 cup gluten free orzo pasta, or substitute with a grain-free option like Banza orzo pasta
1 cup full-fat plain yogurt, or substitute dairy-free yogurt
2/3 cup feta cheese crumbles, or substitute vegan cheese
steps: Cook orzo per package directions. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking process, then set aside.

Meanwhile, make your spicy chickpeas: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and add chickpeas, granulated garlic, salt and cayenne pepper. Toss occasionally and cook until browned (about 5-7 minutes). Remove from heat and set aside.
While pasta and chickpeas cook, make tzatziki sauce: add yogurt through minced garlic to a small mixing bowl and stir until well combined. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
To assemble: add cooked orzo, chickpeas, tzatziki sauce and all remaining ingredients to bowls and garnish with additional dill, and a lemon slice.

name: Noodleless Protein Pad Thai Stirfry
ingredients: 2 tsp cooking fat of choice (olive oil, avocado oil, coconut oil, butter or ghee)
3 eggs, whisked Click here for egg replacements
1 – 1 ¼ lb. chicken breast, cubed into 1 inch pieces (may substitute chicken thighs or ground chicken), or omit the chicken, or substitute meat alternative like tofu, tempeh, or Beyond Ground Beef
1 ½ cup shredded red cabbage (may substitute green cabbage)
1 red bell pepper, seeded and sliced
1 small yellow onion (1 ½ cups, sliced)
6–8 green onions, thinly sliced white/light green parts divide with dark green parts
2 tsp. fresh ginger (may substitute 1/2 tsp. ground ginger*)
Sea salt & black pepper to taste
steps: Place a large skillet (at least a 12-inch skillet) over medium heat. Add 1 teaspoon of the cooking fat (if the skillet is not non-stick, you may need more fat). Once hot, add the whisked eggs and scramble. Remove from the skillet and set aside.

To the skillet, add the additional 1 teaspoon of cooking fat as well as the cubed chicken. Sauté for about 2 minutes. Next add the remaining skillet ingredients, except for the dark green parts of green onion. The skillet will be very full until it cooks down. Continue to cook for 8-12 minutes carefully stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, combine the sauce ingredients and whisk until well combined and smooth.

Once the veggies are tender and cooked down and chicken is cooked through. Add the eggs back to the skillet, the dark green parts of green onion, and the sauce. Stir to combine and allow to cook for another 1-2 minutes or until heated through.
Serve hot topped with cilantro, cashews, sesame seeds, and/or green onion plus a squeeze of fresh lime juice.

name: Turmeric Steel Cut Oats
ingredients: ½ cup steel cut oats (use certified gluten-free if needed)
1 ½ cups water (2 cups for thinner consistency)
1 cup plant-based milk or whole-fat milk
steps: Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the oats and toast for 2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add in the water and milk and mix. Bring to a boil. (about 5 mins)
Reduce heat to medium low. Stir and continue to cook for 10 mins.

Mix in the spices, salt and maple and cook for another 7 minutes or longer until the oats are cooked to preference. Taste and adjust sweet and flavors. The oats will need a few minutes longer if you double/increase the recipe.
There might be some extra liquid in the saucepan. The liquid keeps getting absorbed by the oats as the porridge sits. Cook for a few minutes longer or let it cool for 10-15 minutes to thicken.
Garnish with sliced strawberries, chia seeds, pepita seeds, currants or other fresh or dried fruit of choice. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Turmeric Steel Cut Oats
ingredients: ½ cup gluten-free steel cut oats
1 ½ cups water (2 cups for thinner consistency)
1 cup plant-based milk or whole-fat milk
steps: Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the oats and toast for 2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add in the water and milk and mix. Bring to a boil. (about 5 mins)
Reduce heat to medium low. Stir and continue to cook for 10 mins.

Mix in the spices, salt and maple and cook for another 7 minutes or longer until the oats are cooked to preference. Taste and adjust sweet and flavors. The oats will need a few minutes longer if you double/increase the recipe.
There might be some extra liquid in the saucepan. The liquid keeps getting absorbed by the oats as the porridge sits. Cook for a few minutes longer or let it cool for 10-15 minutes to thicken.
Garnish with sliced strawberries, chia seeds, pepita seeds, currants or other fresh or dried fruit of choice. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Roasted Beets Pesto and Greens Pasta
ingredients: 16 oz gluten free pasta, or substitute a grain-free pasta like Banza
1 cup reserved pasta water from boiling
2-4 cups (loosely packed) beet greens or other mixed greens
¼ cup pumpkin seeds, unsalted (save half for the pesto)
steps: Heat a large pot of water to boiling and cook the pasta until al dente (10 minutes or more). Turn off the heat. Drain the pasta while reserving one cup of the pasta water. Return the pasta to the pot.

While the pasta cooks, transfer all of the Beet Pesto Ingredients to a large food processor and blend until a creamy consistency is achieved. A high-powered blender can be used in small batches if a food processor is not available. Set the pesto aside.

With about 5 minutes remaining for the pasta, heat olive oil in a non-stick pan or skillet on medium hight heat. Sautee the pumpkin seeds for 1-3 minutes. Add the greens and cook until wilted, 3-5 minutes. Turn off the heat.

Add in the the beet pesto (a large spoonful at a time) and the greens to the pot of pasta and stir to combine with tongs or a pasta fork. Add in as much pesto to your liking (you will have extra). Use as much of the reserved pasta water as you prefer to thin out the pesto.
Top with more cheese and pumpkin seeds, optional.

name: Honey Sesame Salmon with Sweet Potato Fries and Avocado
ingredients: 1 tbsp honey, or substitute maple syrup
A few sprinkles of ground ginger (or one sprinkle of fresh grated ginger)
A sprinkle of salt, paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, and black pepper
steps: Cut up the sweet potato and coat with olive oil and seasoning. Place on parchment paper on a baking sheet and cook in the oven for 40 mins at 400F, flipping halfway through.

To make the marinade, combine the coconut aminos, sesame oil, honey, minced garlic, ground ginger, and lime juice. Pour on top of the salmon (saving about ¼) in a baking dish and let sit for at least an hour. 3. Pour the rest of the marinade on top before baking for 15 minutes at 400F. Then broil for 3 minutes.
Top with sliced avocado. If you’d like, serve over a bed of salad greens with a sprinkle of black sesame seeds as garnish.

name: Pumpkin Chia or Oats with Caramelized Banana Slices
ingredients: ⅓ cup rolled oats OR chia seeds
1-2 tsp honey (or maple syrup if you’re vegan)
1 cup full fat milk (or substitute plant-based milk if you’re avoiding dairy)
Optional toppings: nut butter, pumpkin seeds, coconut flakes, cacao nibs, berries
steps: Add all the ingredients to a sauce pan and bring to a low simmer. Allow to cook and thicken as desired, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir well again.

While the oats are simmering, slice the banana into coins. Heat a pan (preferably non-stick) to medium heat. Place bananas on the pan with a drizzle of honey or maple syrup. Allow them to brown on both sides.
Serve the cooked oats topped with hot banana slices and any other toppings you’d like!

name: Massaged Kale Salad
ingredients: 1 large bunch curly green or red kale, washed and patted dry, tough stems removed (about 5 cups)
1 medium carrot, peeled and julienned or cut into ribbons with a vegetable peeler
1 cup shredded Brussels sprouts (may substitute with an additional 2 cups kale)
¼ cup raisins or dried cranberries
3 tbsp olive oil 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar
Optional 1/3 cup feta, goat or parmesan cheese (omit for vegan or dairy-free)
steps: Get out a big bowl. Tear the washed kale into bite-sized pieces and toss into the bowl. Sprinkle with sea salt.
Massage the kale. Really get into it. Continue to massage the leaves with the salt until they start to soften and wilt a bit.
Combine the oil, vinegar, and black pepper and set aside. Do not add extra salt, there’s plenty on the kale already. Toss remaining ingredients (apple through sunflower seeds) into kale.
Drizzle with oil and vinegar dressing, toss to combine and serve.

name: Zucchini and Chorizo Enchilada Skillet
ingredients: 1 lb chicken or turkey chorizo, or substitute tofu or a ground meat alternative like Beyond Ground Beef
1 cup enchilada sauce (use one that is gluten free and has low or no added sugar)
1 cup shredded cheddar cheese, or substitute chopped avocado or a vegan cheese
steps: Saute Chorizo until almost cooked all the way through, about 6-7 minutes. Meanwhile chop your zucchini into quartered pieces. Cut tortillas into 1/2 inch wide pieces.
Preheat broiler. Add in enchilada sauce, zucchini, and tortillas. Cook for an additional 3-5 minutes.
If using cheese, top the mixture with shredded cheese and place in oven to broil for 3-4 minutes. If not using cheese, top with avocado and serve.

name: Loaded Toast
ingredients: Spread options: avocado, cottage cheese, nut butter

Toppings options: nuts, seeds, coconut flakes, berries, slice banana, cacao nibs or dark chocolate pieces, pomegranate seeds, roasted chickpeas, capers, smoked salmon, fried egg*, sliced onions, sliced tomatoes, basil
steps: Toast 1 or 2 slices of bread. Then spread avocado, cottage cheese, or nut butter liberally over the toast. Cover with toppings of your choice.
